based_on: Byte-precise mutation of fixtures/xor-per-neuron-bias.golden.jsonl. Differs in exactly 4 places: the 3 fixture_status fields and the single targeted updates[0].kind string-enum field.

what_was_NOT_mutated: updates[0].layer_edge (still 'input_to_hidden' — also contradicts kind='bias' but Rule 0 fires on kind/role first)
updates[0].optimizer.factors (still 2 factors — weight-style; bias updates have 1 factor)
updates[1..8] (all other updates retain their correct kind)
topology, parameters_before, parameters_after, forward, loss, backward

single_target_invariant: Receipt declares updates[0].kind='bias' on parameter w_x1_h1 which has topology role='input_to_hidden_weight'. Per Rule 0f: kind='bias' requires role in {hidden_bias, output_bias}; kind='weight' requires role outside that set. The pairing is contradictory.
purpose: Anti-circularity (Csmith/CompCert lineage) for the v0.4.1 Rule 0 structural-consistency reconciler: kind-role mismatch is a class of authoring error that numeric Rules 1-8 cannot catch (the rules don't consult topology.parameters[].role). Without this Phase 0 check, a receipt claiming a weight-role parameter received a bias-style update would pass numerically and silently misclassify the parameter type.
